task_completion.description: Create the 'EC2 Compute Tier' graph using the correct CPU utilization metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'EC2 Compute Tier' is visible in the Graphite dashboard. The legend or metric tree indicates it is using the 'servers.ec2_instance_*' CPU utilization metrics.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Database Tier' graph using the correct RDS CPU utilization metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Database Tier' is visible in the Graphite dashboard. The legend or metric tree indicates it is using the 'servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization' metric.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Load Balancer Tier' graph using the derivative function on the requests count metric.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Load Balancer Tier' is visible. The metric path or legend explicitly shows the 'derivative()' function applied to the 'servers.load_balancer.requests.count' metric.

task_completion.description: Create the 'Storage Tier' graph using the correct disk write bytes metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Storage Tier' is visible in the Graphite dashboard. The legend or metric tree indicates it is using the 'servers.ec2_instance_*.disk.write_bytes' metrics.

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard with the name 'Infrastructure Health'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web interface shows the dashboard title as 'Infrastructure Health' at the top of the screen, indicating it has been successfully saved with the correct name.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to build the dashboard rather than writing a script or JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Graphite web UI (e.g., metric tree, Composer, dashboard interface) rather than using a terminal or text editor to generate the dashboard.

integrity.description: The graphs are genuinely rendered by the Graphite application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs display actual plotted data lines and standard Graphite UI elements (axes, legends, background), not just static text or mocked images.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's address bar shows a local URL corresponding to the Graphite web application, and the interface is fully loaded.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is 'Infrastructure Health'. The dashboard must contain exactly four graphs with the following exact titles: 'EC2 Compute Tier', 'Database Tier', 'Load Balancer Tier', and 'Storage Tier'. The 'Load Balancer Tier' graph must explicitly use the 'derivative' function on its metric.
